name: SimpleValueChecker
javadoc:
/** * Simple implementation of the {@link ConvergenceChecker} interface using * only objective function values. * * Convergence is considered to have been reached if either the relative * difference between the objective function values is smaller than a * threshold or if either the absolute difference between the objective * function values is smaller than another threshold. *
* The {@link #converged(int,PointValuePair,PointValuePair) converged} * method will also return {@code true} if the number of iterations has been set * (see {@link #SimpleValueChecker(double,double,int) this constructor}). * * @deprecated As of 3.1 (to be removed in 4.0). * @since 3.0 */

members.name: SimpleValueChecker
SimpleValueChecker
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Build an instance with specified thresholds.
 *
 * In order to perform only relative checks, the absolute tolerance
 * must be set to a negative value. In order to perform only absolute
 * checks, the relative tolerance must be set to a negative value.
 *
 * @param relativeThreshold relative tolerance threshold
 * @param absoluteThreshold absolute tolerance threshold
 */

members.name: SimpleValueChecker
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Builds an instance with specified thresholds.
 *
 * In order to perform only relative checks, the absolute tolerance
 * must be set to a negative value. In order to perform only absolute
 * checks, the relative tolerance must be set to a negative value.
 *
 * @param relativeThreshold relative tolerance threshold
 * @param absoluteThreshold absolute tolerance threshold
 * @param maxIter Maximum iteration count.
 * @throws NotStrictlyPositiveException if {@code maxIter <= 0}.
 *
 * @since 3.1
 */

members.name: converged
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Check if the optimization algorithm has converged considering the
 * last two points.
 * This method may be called several time from the same algorithm
 * iteration with different points. This can be detected by checking the
 * iteration number at each call if needed. Each time this method is
 * called, the previous and current point correspond to points with the
 * same role at each iteration, so they can be compared. As an example,
 * simplex-based algorithms call this method for all points of the simplex,
 * not only for the best or worst ones.
 *
 * @param iteration Index of current iteration
 * @param previous Best point in the previous iteration.
 * @param current Best point in the current iteration.
 * @return {@code true} if the algorithm has converged.
 */

tags.name: previous
tags.content: Best point in the previous iteration.
tags.description: Best point in the previous iteration.

tags.name: current
tags.content: Best point in the current iteration.
tags.description: Best point in the current iteration.

tags.content: true} if the algorithm has converged.
